purpose: Fault-injection countermeasure coverage ledger. A single induced fault (a skipped branch, a flipped bit, a glitched verify) can turn an invalid signature into an emitted 'success' or leak a nonce (Boneh-DeMillo-Lipton / differential fault analysis). The standard countermeasure is sign-then-verify (FIPS 186-4): re-verify the signature you just produced before returning it, so a fault during signing is caught by the verify. This ledger lists every critical signing path, its fault countermeasure, and the fault-injection probe that exercises it. The gate enforces (1) the countermeasure function still exists AND re-verifies (not a hollow stub), and (2) the fault probe is wired into the audit runner. Enforced by ci/check_fault_countermeasure_coverage.py.
methodology: For each signing path: confirm a sign-then-verify (or equivalent redundant-computation) countermeasure is present and actually re-verifies, and that a fault-injection test drives it. A countermeasure that was deleted or hollowed out (no verify call) is a silent regression that re-opens the fault-forgery surface.

paths.claim: ecdsa_sign_verified re-verifies the produced signature before returning it; a fault during k^-1/scalar-mul yields a sig that fails the internal verify and is rejected.
schnorr_sign_verified re-verifies the produced BIP-340 signature before returning it; a fault during s=k+e*d is caught by the internal verify.
Two deterministic signatures over the same (key, msg) that differ reveal a fault; the DFA probe asserts a single induced fault does not enable key recovery / forged success.
A glitched deterministic signature is detected; the DFA probe asserts no forged-success path.
A fault during partial signing must not yield a partial sig that passes musig2_partial_verify / frost_verify_partial for a different secret — a partial-verify-after-sign countermeasure.
